title: Stay Sharp: - Double-Check: Look up Facts You Think You
subtitle: Fight the Illusory Truth Effect
description:
To stay sharp:
- Double-check: Look up facts you think you "just know."
- Ask questions: Challenge statements that feel familiar but unverified.
- Don’t repeat unverified info: Sharing it reinforces the bias.

Example: Before you repeat a fun “fact” you read online, spend 5 minutes confirming it.

faq.answer: Research shows habits can take 21-66 days to form. Focus on consistency rather than perfection, and use the app to build momentum.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Log every familiar claim you encounter for three days, noting whether you checked it. This will establish your starting point for verification habits.

howTo.title: Practice the 5-minute check
howTo.summary: Select 3 to 6 familiar claims each week and spend 5 minutes verifying each one using reliable sources. This builds your verification routine.

howTo.title: Introduce the verification norm
howTo.summary: Share the 5-minute verification practice with a group, encouraging others to check claims together. This fosters a culture of accuracy.

howTo.title: Reflect on your progress
howTo.summary: At the end of each week, review your checks and note any changes in your beliefs or conversations. This reinforces learning.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your verification results in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and insights. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/fight-illusory-truth-effect/.
